title: vandellia pusilla
intro: Vandellia pusilla, also known as the Indian bloodroot or kutki, is a plant species used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Vandellia pusilla include treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as helping to manage diabetes and improve liver function. Herbal preparations of the plant include decoctions, infusions, and powders, which can be used to create teas, capsules, and topical creams for medicinal purposes.
